# backend/routers/evals_cost.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-13T10:03:11Z

## Purpose
§68.9 Cost eval router.

4 endpoints under /api/v1/insur/evals/cost/* federated via core.insur_audit
(surface=evals_cost). Sibling of §68.8 functional + §68.10 safety; RBAC
catch-all `/api/v1/insur/evals/*` already gates this surface.

Composes with §38.3 + §41.1 (FinOps) + §41.3 (tenant) + §57.7 + §68.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `typing` import Any
- from `fastapi` import APIRouter, HTTPException, Query, Request
- from `core.insur_audit` import log_insur_access
- from `services` import cost_eval_service

## Inputs
(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Functions
- `cost_global(http_request)`: Total cost across all tenants/models — 24h / 7d / 30d + all-time.
- `cost_by_model(http_request, since)`: Per-model cost ranking, highest-cost first.
- `cost_by_request(http_request, request_id)`: Single-request cost detail.
- `cost_per_tenant(http_request, tenant_id, since)`: Per-tenant cost breakdown with per-model totals within the tenant.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Literal sub-paths BEFORE parameterized to dodge §66.3 greedy-match trap.
- Parameterized LAST.
